fixes_applied: Replaced tests/test.sh: was the SWE-rebench auto-generated stub. Because tests/install_config.json has FAIL_TO_PASS=[], the stub fell back to 'passed/total' of every test in `go test -v ./...` against the unmodified base commit. The base commit (84fda9c8) builds clean and the metricstore tests pass — so a no-op agent scored ~0.99, making the task pure leaderboard noise (confirmed by the v0.4.3 trial reward distribution all ≥0.99). New test.sh runs 7 inline behavioral/structural Python regex gates against the patched cc-backend repo, builds /logs/verifier/gates.json, and emits a weighted-replace reward.

Rewrote tests/test_manifest.yaml: replaced the previous 11-gate manifest (STRUCTURAL + ANTI_STUB + P2P_REGRESSION decoration that test.sh ignored entirely because the SWE-rebench stub never reads test_manifest.yaml) with 7 F2P gates aligned 1:1 to the new test.sh inline gates, weight sum 1.00.

All gates use behavioral language and regex on the public surface (struct field names, JSON schema property keys, function-body markers) — implementation-agnostic: F2P_CHECKPOINT_HARDCODED accepts any const name as long as `12 * time.Hour` appears and Keys.Checkpoints.Interval is gone; F2P_CLEANUP_USES_RETENTION accepts ≥1 Keys.RetentionInMemory reference; F2P_SCHEMA_NO_INTERVAL only checks the absence of 'interval' property+required entries (multiple equivalent schema reformulations pass); F2P_DEFAULT_WAL scopes to the Keys var block. F2P_SOURCE_FILES_MODIFIED (anti-stub coverage) requires ≥5 of 6 declared source_files to differ from base commit 84fda9c8 — guards against agents who edit only one file.
f2p_added: F2P_STRUCTS_NO_INTERVAL (weight 0.20) — Checkpoints and Cleanup structs in pkg/metricstore/config.go no longer declare `Interval string`
F2P_DEFAULT_WAL (weight 0.10) — Keys default Checkpoints.FileFormat is 'wal', not 'json'

F2P_SCHEMA_NO_INTERVAL (weight 0.20) — pkg/metricstore/configSchema.go: 'interval' property removed from checkpoints/cleanup; top-level required does not list 'checkpoints'; no `required` array contains 'interval'
F2P_CHECKPOINT_HARDCODED (weight 0.15) — pkg/metricstore/checkpoint.go Checkpointing() has `12 * time.Hour` and no `Keys.Checkpoints.Interval` reference
F2P_CLEANUP_USES_RETENTION (weight 0.15) — pkg/metricstore/archive.go CleanUp() passes Keys.RetentionInMemory and never references Keys.Cleanup.Interval
F2P_CONFIGS_NO_INTERVAL (weight 0.10) — configs/config.json and configs/config-demo.json metric-store blocks contain no 'interval' key
F2P_SOURCE_FILES_MODIFIED (weight 0.10) — anti-stub: ≥5 of 6 declared source_files differ from base commit 84fda9c8
validation_method: Cloned ClusterCockpit/cc-backend at parent commit 84fda9c8 to /tmp/cc-backend-test, applied canonical patch from data-pipeline/artifacts_cc-backend/canonical_patches/ceb68597-9f77-4ffe-9a4b-6c7c44e7f3bb.json, and ran tests/test.sh via local wrapper (REPO=/tmp/cc-backend-test override) against (a) clean baseline, (b) canonical-patch-applied state, and (c) partial-fix where only 3 of 6 files were patched.

baseline.note: Every gate FAILs as expected — Interval fields present, FileFormat defaults to json, schema requires checkpoints+interval, Checkpointing parses Keys.Checkpoints.Interval, CleanUp uses Keys.Cleanup.Interval, configs contain interval, no source files modified.

canonical_patched.note: All 7 gates PASS. Discrimination spread = 1.000 (baseline 0.000 → patched 1.000).

partial_3_of_6_files.note: Half-credit for half-job — partial fixes get partial reward, no all-or-nothing cliff.

notes: The canonical patch is rich and testable: 80 deletions, 27 additions across 6 files, a config-simplification refactor with crisp before/after states. Every change has a public, regex-detectable footprint, so I leaned entirely on regex/text checks (no Go AST, no `go build`/`go test` invocation in the verifier — those would add Go-toolchain dependency at verify time and would add ~30-60s to each replay). The existing metricstore tests would still pass against the patched code (they don't reference removed fields) and `go build` would succeed because the canonical patch is internally consistent — both can be added as P2P_REGRESSION (informational) gates if needed. allow_internet is correctly under [environment] in task.toml. Reward formula is standard weighted-replace per CLAUDE.md / scoring_traps.md (sum_weights=1.00 → inner_share=0; existing base_reward fully replaced by F2P weights, no double-counting). p2p_failed hardcoded False.
